title: The number I am most afraid to write down
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming the wealth ceiling and examining what keeps it in place.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 24 of the MONEY program. Use to find the specific wealth figure that still feels impossible and write directly into the reasons it feels that way.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to examine the exact figure where your sense of financial possibility cuts out, and what sits behind that ceiling.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 24 of the MONEY program. The prompts go after the specific figure you haven't written yet: the number that triggers impossibility rather than calm expectation. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
If I fully believed I was worthy of millions, what decisions would I make differently right now?
Write the decisions. Specific ones, not attitudes.
What existing abundance in my life proves I am already capable of receiving more?
What is the number I am most afraid to write down?
Write it. Just the digits. Then write whatever comes next.
What does that figure feel like in my body when I look at it?
Name the physical sensation, not the emotion word.
Whose voice tells me that number isn't mine? When did I first hear it?
Trace the voice. Name it plainly.
Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
